title: Shakshuka Poached Eggs Tomato

seo.title: Shakshuka Recipe: Poached Eggs in Tomato Sauce
seo.metaDescription: Master the authentic Shakshuka recipe with perfectly poached eggs in a rich, spicy tomato sauce. A delicious Middle Eastern breakfast or brunch idea!
seo.h1: Authentic Shakshuka Recipe with Poached Eggs
h2Headings: Tips for Perfect Poached Eggs in Shakshuka
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Shakshuka recipe with perfectly poached eggs in a rich tomato sauce, garnished with fresh herbs.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy this delicious Shakshuka recipe, a classic Middle Eastern breakfast with poached eggs and a flavorful tomato base.

description: Shakshuka is a vibrant and flavorful Middle Eastern breakfast, featuring perfectly poached eggs nestled in a rich, simmering sauce of ripe tomatoes, bell peppers, and aromatic spices. This hearty dish is a delightful way to start your day, offering warmth and a burst of savory goodness with every spoonful. It's truly a celebration of simple, fresh ingredients coming together in a harmonious and comforting meal.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: Heat the olive oil in a large, oven-safe skillet or pan over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and diced bell peppers. Cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables have softened.

instructions.title: Add Garlic and Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the minced garlic, ground cumin, smoked paprika, and cayenne pepper (if using). Cook for another 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the spices are fragrant. Be careful not to burn the garlic.

instructions.title: Build the Tomato Base
instructions.text: Pour in the canned crushed tomatoes and stir in the tomato paste. Season generously with salt and black pepper. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, stirring to combine all ingredients.

instructions.title: Simmer the Sauce
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to low, cover the skillet, and let the sauce simmer for 15-20 minutes. This allows the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Poach the Eggs
instructions.text: Carefully create four small indentations or wells in the simmering tomato sauce using the back of a spoon. Gently crack one egg into each well. Cover the skillet again and cook for 5-8 minutes, or until the egg whites are set but the yolks are still runny, to your preference.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Once the eggs are cooked to your liking, remove the skillet from the heat. Garnish generously with freshly chopped parsley or cilantro. Serve immediately, directly from the pan, with warm pita bread or crusty bread for dipping.

tips: For an extra layer of flavor, roast the bell peppers before dicing and adding them to the sauce. This adds a smoky depth that is truly authentic.
Do not overcrowd the pan when poaching eggs. If making for a larger crowd, consider using a wider pan or cooking in batches to ensure even cooking of the eggs.
Serve your Shakshuka with a side of labneh (strained yogurt) or crumbled feta cheese for a creamy, tangy contrast that complements the rich tomato sauce beautifully.
article: Shakshuka Poached Eggs Tomato is a beloved Middle Eastern Breakfast Recipes dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
